title: salix repens var. dunensis
intro: Salix repens var. dunensis, also known as creeping willow, is a type of willow tree that is native to the UK and Ireland. It is a shrub-like tree with slender branches and small, dark green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Salix repens var. dunensis include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and promoting relaxation, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or salve for topical use.
